responses: Hello! I am MedBot. How can I help you today?
Hi there! Tell me your symptoms and I will help you.
Hey! I am here to help with your health queries.
Goodbye! Stay healthy!
Take care! Visit a doctor if symptoms persist.
Bye! Remember to stay hydrated and rest well.
You're welcome! Stay healthy!
Happy to help! Consult a doctor for serious issues.
Glad I could help!

For fever:
- Take Paracetamol (Crocin/Dolo 650) every 6 hours
- Drink plenty of water and fluids
- Rest and apply cold compress on forehead
- Avoid oily and spicy food
⚠️ See a doctor if fever exceeds 103°F or lasts more than 3 days.

Suggested medicines for fever:
- Paracetamol (Dolo 650 / Crocin)
- Stay hydrated with ORS or coconut water
- Wear light clothing
⚠️ Consult a doctor if fever is very high or accompanied by rash.

For cold:
- Cetirizine (Cetzine) for runny nose
- Nasal drops like Nasivion for blocked nose
- Steam inhalation twice a day
- Drink warm water with honey and ginger
⚠️ If cold lasts more than a week, consult a doctor.

For cough:
- Dry cough: Honitus syrup or Benadryl
- Wet cough: Alex or Ascoril syrup
- Sip warm water with honey and tulsi
- Avoid cold drinks and ice cream
⚠️ If coughing blood or cough lasts more than 2 weeks, see a doctor immediately.

For headache:
- Take Paracetamol (Crocin) or Ibuprofen (Brufen)
- For migraine: Saridon or Sumo tablet
- Rest in a dark quiet room
- Apply cold or warm compress on forehead
- Stay hydrated
⚠️ Seek emergency care if headache is sudden and severe.

For sore throat:
- Strepsils or Cofsils lozenges for relief
- Gargle with warm salt water 3 times a day
- Betadine gargle mouthwash
- Drink warm honey-lemon water
⚠️ If throat is very swollen or you have high fever, visit a doctor for antibiotics.

For stomach pain:
- Antacid: Digene or Gelusil syrup
- Gas/bloating: Pudin Hara or Eno
- Spasms: Cyclopam tablet
- Avoid spicy and oily food
- Drink warm water
⚠️ If pain is severe or accompanied by vomiting/blood, go to a doctor immediately.

For nausea and vomiting:
- Ondansetron (Emeset) tablet or syrup
- Domperidone (Domstal) before meals
- Sip small amounts of water frequently
- Eat plain foods like khichdi or toast
- Drink ORS to prevent dehydration
⚠️ If vomiting persists more than 24 hours, see a doctor.

For diarrhea:
- ORS (Electral) to prevent dehydration
- Loperamide (Imodium) to reduce motions
- Norflox-TZ tablet (consult doctor first)
- Eat BRAT diet: Banana, Rice, Applesauce, Toast
- Avoid dairy and spicy food
⚠️ If diarrhea lasts more than 2 days or has blood, visit a doctor.

For diabetes management:
- Take prescribed medicines like Metformin regularly
- Monitor blood sugar daily
- Avoid sugar, white rice, and processed food
- Exercise at least 30 minutes daily
- Eat high fiber foods like vegetables and whole grains
⚠️ Never change diabetes medicine without consulting your doctor.

For high BP:
- Take prescribed medicines like Amlodipine or Telmisartan
- Reduce salt intake
- Avoid stress and do yoga/meditation
- Monitor BP regularly

For low BP:
- Drink ORS or salt-sugar water
- Lie down and elevate legs
- Eat small frequent meals
⚠️ Always consult a doctor before taking BP medicines.

For acidity:
- Antacids: Gelusil, Digene, or Mucaine gel
- PPI: Omeprazole (Omez) or Pantoprazole (Pan-D)
- Avoid spicy, oily, and fried food
- Eat smaller meals and don't lie down after eating
- Drink cold milk for instant relief
⚠️ If acidity is severe or frequent, consult a gastroenterologist.

For skin allergy:
- Antihistamine: Cetirizine (Cetzine) or Loratadine
- Calamine lotion for rash and itching
- Hydrocortisone cream for inflammation
- Avoid the allergen (dust, pollen, certain foods)
- Keep skin moisturized
⚠️ If rash spreads rapidly or you have difficulty breathing, go to emergency immediately.

For back pain:
- Ibuprofen (Brufen) or Diclofenac (Voveran) for pain relief
- Muscle relaxant: Thiocolchicoside (Myospaz)
- Apply Volini or Moov gel on affected area
- Rest but avoid prolonged bed rest
- Do gentle stretches and hot fomentation
⚠️ See a doctor if pain radiates to legs or is accompanied by numbness.

For eye irritation:
- Use Refresh Tears or similar lubricating eye drops
- For infection: Moxifloxacin eye drops (Moxicip)
- For allergy: Olopatadine eye drops (Pataday)
- Avoid touching or rubbing eyes
- Wash eyes with clean cool water
⚠️ If vision is blurred or there is severe pain, see an eye doctor immediately.

I am MedBot, a health assistant chatbot! I can help you with:
- Identifying symptoms
- Suggesting common medicines
- General health advice
⚠️ I am not a replacement for a real doctor. Always consult a medical professional for serious issues.
